name: Crisp Roasted Pig's Head
recipeIngredient: 1 Pig's head, cleaned and tongue removed
1/2 cup Mien see (ground brown bean sauce) or 1/2 cup oyster sauce
1 cup Honey, combined with 1 cup Boiling water
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any hair on the head by singeing over an open flame or plucking.
2. Scrub well using a vegetable brush (if desired), then sprinkle with salt and rub it into the skin.
3. Rinse thoroughly with cool water and pat dry. Remove any excess fat.
4. Place the head in a colander in the sink and pour a kettle full of boiling water over it. Let cool.
5. Combine the 5-spice powder, salt, bean sauce (or oyster sauce), bourbon, honey, and boiling water mixture.
6. Slash the meat on the underside of the head and rub half of the spice mixture into the meat.
7. Rub the remaining spice mixture onto the skin.
8. Place the head upright on a rack in a large baking pan.
9. Bake at 375°F for 1 1/2 hours.
10. Lower the heat to 325°F and continue cooking for an additional 2 hours, or until the meat is cooked through. Baste the skin well every 30 minutes with the honey-water mixture.
- If ears begin to brown too quickly during the cooking period, wrap them with foil.
11. When the head is done, remove it to a platter and garnish with watercress or coriander.
12. Chop the head into pieces and serve with sweet vegetable relish or plum sauce.
[I'd definitely serve with spiced salt and Chinese mustard and minced green onions for dipping too.]
